title: podocarpus latifolius subsp. latior
intro: Podocarpus latifolius subsp. latior, also known as the Yew Pine, is a species of evergreen coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Podocarpus latifolius subsp. latior is valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal benefits. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and wood of Podocarpus latifolius subsp. latior are often used to make teas, infusions, and other remedies.
